response.justification: A capable senior generalist, but a poor fit for a hands-on C++ capital-markets role. The only C++ experience is roughly six months at National Instruments in 2014–2015 plus a university thesis—C++ is not even in the listed skills, and the last decade is TypeScript/Node.js/DLang web and platform work. There is no Windows-platform C++, no SQL Server/Oracle, and no fixed-income, investment-banking, or capital-markets domain exposure (RIDE Capital is consumer investment fintech, not capital markets). Transferable low-latency/concurrency and CI/CD craftsmanship exist but do not cover the core mandatory requirements.
strengths: Strong low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience
Solid software craftsmanship: TDD, pairing, CI/CD, trunk-based
Some prior C/C++ exposure at National Instruments and thesis work
concerns: C++ experience is minimal and ~10 years dated; not a listed skill
No SQL Server/Oracle or Windows-platform development shown
